observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct annual inventory-taking of all
PPE and submit the RPCPPE in the prescribed form based on the actual
physical count conducted within the prescribed period;

b. Require the GSO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain
the property records for all PPE and undertake the required reconciliation
of the Property and Accounting records thereafter;

c. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: i) ensure monthly recognition of
depreciation expense of all PPE accounts and record the adjustments in the
government equity account for the prior years’ depreciation expenses; ii)
immediately transfer the completed PPE recorded in the TF to the GF; and
iii) make the necessary adjusting entries in the books to correct the identified
misclassified PPE to come up with the correct PPE accounts balances; and

d. Facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the
implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that Management:

a. Demand the immediate settlement/liquidation of outstanding CAs,
particularly from the Municipal Treasurer and those who are still connected
with the LGU, pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002;

b. Stop the practice of granting CA to officers and employees without a legally
authorized specific purpose, and instead directly issue checks for the
payment of operating expenses;

c. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: i) strictly monitor the granting
and liquidation of CAs and ensure that no additional CA is granted unless
the previous CA is fully liquidated or properly accounted for, in order to
avoid undue accumulation of outstanding CAs; and ii) prepare and maintain
an SL for each CA and record the liquidation specifically to their
corresponding CA to avoid overlapping and improper offsetting of
accounts; and

d. Conform with the procedures and requirements set forth in COA Circular
No. 2016-005 and immediately facilitate the request for write-off of dormant
unliquidated CAs, where applicable.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all
supplies and materials on hand including the semi-expendable properties,
submit the required RPCI and RPCSP and coordinate with the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the same with the accounting records;

b. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchase of
inventories in the appropriate inventory accounts and the issuance,
consumption and distribution thereof as expense upon receipt of RIS and
SSMI of a given period and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the
correct balance of the affected inventory and government equity accounts;
and

c. Require the GSO and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to properly maintain,
update and reconcile the SCs and SLCs, respectively. Also, strengthen
controls over the semi-expendable properties by establishing and
maintaining accurate records, reports, and registries as required by COA
Circular No. 2022-004.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention to the
timely preparation and submission of the required BRS for all accounts;

b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to: i) consistently update all cashbooks to
ensure that the data reflected therein is accurate and can be reliably used in
the preparation of a complete BRS; ii) closely monitor the bank account
balances to ensure compliance with the required minimum ADB and avoid
further service charges; and iii) coordinate with the depository bank-DBP,
to negotiate possible waivers of service charges, particularly if the LGU
maintains a long-standing relationship or significant deposits; and

c. Instruct both the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
prioritize the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts to establish an
accurate and reliable balances to be reflected in the FS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Exert more effort to trace and verify records related to unreconciled and
non-moving accounts, facilitate the return of any unutilized funds for
completed/cancelled projects to the concerned SAs and take up the necessary
correcting/adjusting entries in the books for the fair presentation of the said
account in the FS;

b. Prepare confirmation letters for the SAs to verify any outstanding
receivables from the Municipality; and

c. Maintain an appropriate and complete SLs, including detailed schedules to
ensure accurate monitoring of balances under the Due to NGAs account and
to establish clear traceability of PPAs, thereby preventing subsequent
discrepancies.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

a. Establish individual SLs for each loan and its corresponding project
components and ensure that each SL reflects the specific details of the loan,
including the date and amount of release, principal and interest payments,
project expenditures and amortization schedules;

b. Review and reclassify loan-related balances at year-end by properly
identifying the current portion of the long-term liabilities and presenting it
under the correct classification in the Statement of Financial Position; and

c. Record Interest Expense and Other Financial Charges, such as GRT, under
their respective accounts to ensure accurate classification and reporting of
financial transactions.

We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: (i) issue immediately the checks for
each LGU at an amount equivalent to their respective RPT and CTC shares
and (ii) ensure the prompt and regular remittance of the shares of concerned
LGUs within five days after the end of each quarter; and

b. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to consistently notify the LGUs of
their respective RPT and CTC share amounts each quarter to enable them
to claim the same from the Treasury Office.

We recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
conduct a detailed review of the accounts and make the necessary
adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the
affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the
Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts
are at all times the same.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

a. Strictly monitor the conduct of regular and periodic verification, analysis
and validation of the existence of payable accounts to ensure that only valid
payables are recorded in the books; and

b. Prepare a certified schedule of aging of dormant Accounts Payable accounts
for which there are no valid claimants/creditors and no supporting
documents, thereafter, revert the verified amounts to the Unappropriated
Surplus of the Government Equity Account by preparing the necessary
Journal Entry Vouchers as basis for recording in the books of accounts of
the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the LSB to ensure that the prescribed planning and budgeting tools
are prepared, submitted and effectively utilized and that the timelines in the
planning and budgeting process are strictly observed as prescribed under
the aforementioned Circulars. Further, include the previous years’
unexpended balances and continuing appropriations, in determining the net
amount available for appropriation, incorporate in the SEF budget PPAs
intended for ECCD and ensure that the SEF Budget conforms with the
prescribed format;

b. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Item 5.4 of the said Circular by securing
a duly approved LSB Resolution prior to the implementation of the SEF
Budget and; and

c. Comply with the mandatory reporting requirements by submitting the
Quarterly and Annual SEF Utilization Reports and immediately submit the
corresponding DVs and other supporting documents pertaining to the
utilization of SEF for further audit verification.

We recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the lacking DVs with complete
supporting documents as well as the unsubmitted cash disbursement and collection
accounts for CY 2024. Strict adherence with the reglementary periods in the rendition
of accounts with complete supporting documents and necessary financial reports,
schedules and financial statements is also enjoined.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team all the aforementioned cancelled and unused
old checks for validation and henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to
the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word
“Cancelled” clearly indicated therein. Likewise, prudence must be exercised in
encoding the details of the checks in order to avoid wastage of checks/resources.

We recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
immediately prepare/keep updated subsidiary records for assets, receivables, liabilities
government equity and other accounts to support the controlling accounts in the GL.
This will facilitate the review and verification of the Audit Team to ensure the
correctness of balances in the FS. Further, best effort must be exerted to trace out and
analyze the details of the previous years’ accounts.

We recommended that Management:

a. Conduct a thorough review and verification of the Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-
IBIG and PhilHealth accounts, perform reconciliation between the Ledgers
and the Schedules of Remittances, make necessary adjustments in the books
to reflect accurate balances and remit immediately all overdue obligations
to prevent further accumulation and avoid potential penalties, if warranted;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to review or trace the cause of the
negative balance of the Due to BIR account, determine the necessary
adjusting entries and update the affected ledger to present an accurate and
reliable balance in the FS. Since Management has acknowledged the
occurrence of over-remittance to BIR, it is also recommended to coordinate
with the Agency and comply with the all the necessary documentary
requirements to facilitate the proper processing of such claims; and

c. Evaluate the current system of withholding and remitting taxes, premiums,
contributions and other deductions to prevent under or over remittance
thereof.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the amount of
₱20,000,000.00 to the TF account and require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and submit SLs for each barangay and projects to be implemented
in order to properly monitor the utilization of the funds intended for the
implementation of these projects;

b. Fast track the implementation of the downloaded projects and ensure that
the eligible programs and projects are completed within the prescribed
timelines; and

c. Prepare and submit the following:

i. Supplemental LDIP and AIP which include the projects funded by the
LGSF and Unprogrammed Appropriations - SBDP NTF-ELCAC; and

ii. Quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project
Implementation using the prescribed format.

We recommended that Management:

a. Ensure timely preparation of the AIP to support and guide the
implementation of PPAs funded under the 20 percent DF and submit a copy
of the AIP to the Audit Team for validation and evaluation;

b. Stop the use of generic or lump sum description in project titles and
appropriations by clearly identifying and itemizing each PPA. Further,
submit the specific details and documentation pertaining to the lump sum
appropriation and utilization of the project Land Improvements;

c. Immediately submit all DVs and its complete supporting documents related
to the implementation of the 20 percent DF to facilitate the conduct of audit
and validation of the regularity and propriety of the transactions; and

d. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to
reconcile the discrepancy between the Utilization Report and the SAAOB
and make the necessary adjustments to ensure the accuracy and reliability
of the financial reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. In coordination with the HRMO, prepare a staff pattern plan for non-
permanent employees and review and evaluate the existing manpower
resources to ensure adequate staffing pattern that suit the present needs of
the offices and create clear-cut policies and guidelines to address the issue
on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs/holders of COS;

b. Direct the Heads of the Municipal Offices to review the DTRs of the JOs
and/or COS personnel under their respective offices before approving the
same to ensure that pertinent supporting documents were duly
accomplished and signed by the employees concerned; and

c. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking
supporting documents and explain the noted deficiencies. Henceforth,
ensure that all payments to JOs and casual employees are properly
supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that Management require the:

a. MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Annual LDRRMFIP which includes
under a separate caption, the list of PPAs charged to the unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years and specify and elaborate the details of its
DRRM program for proper accounting, monitoring and audit of the
LDRRMF;
a. Municipal Treasurer to immediately open a separate bank account for the
LDRRMF- Special Trust Fund (STF). Thereafter, in coordination with the
OIC-Municipal Accountant, transfer the actual cash corresponding to the
unutilized balances of the LDRRMF, both from prior and current years.
Likewise, ensure that the year-end transfer of such unutilized balances is
undertaken regularly in subsequent years and that all related transactions
are properly recorded in their appropriate accounts; and
b. OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate RAAOs for LDRRMF
to facilitate proper control and monitoring of appropriations, allotments
and obligations under the said fund. Additionally, direct the immediate
submission of the RAAOs covering the months of July to November, which
were not made available during the audit for verification.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that the allocation for GAD-related
PPAs be at least five percent of the total annual budget of the LGU;

b. Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO)/
GFPS Focal Person, in coordination with the MPDC, to facilitate the
gathering of data and information needed for the creation of an updated and
well-established GAD database to have an accurate and reliable basis in the
planning and programming of PPAs, as well as in the policy formulation, in
order to properly address the existing and emerging GAD issues and
concerns; and

c. Submit the documents required as attachments to the GAD AR to facilitate
monitoring and evaluation of the GAD PPAs implemented during the year.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Collecting Officers to remit their collections daily and intact to the Municipal Treasurer, otherwise, impose appropriate actions to compel them to diligently observe their obligation; and
Strict compliance to the provisions of Section 69 of PD No.1445 and Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-382 must be observed.

We recommended that Management:
a.	Strictly comply with the submission of report on information and publicity of LGU PPAs as provided in COA Circular 2013-004; and
b.	Proper coordination and reconciliation by the concerned LGU Officials particularly the OIC-Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant, must be made in order to facilitate the preparation and submission of the required reports to the COA within due date.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that the unspent current year LDRRMF amounting to ₱481,876.50 must be transferred to STF-DRRM; and
Ensure that the transfer of cash for the unspent DRRM balances which includes only the QRF and MF-MOOE must be made at the end of the year in accordance with Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management:
Require the LSB to strictly comply with the provisions of DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and Section 272 of RA No. 7160, such as:
Formulate a three-year program indicating strategic prioritization policies in the allocation of the SEF to schools;
Formulate and issue budget prioritization policies and tentative budget ceilings of the Municipality thru a Budget Call;
Submit the quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO; and
Post the utilization report of the SEF Budget in the website of the LGU and/or in at least three (3) conspicuous public places.
Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to LSB actual SEF Income – quarterly, annual for the preceding and issuing year, and submit to COA the quarterly and annual SEF Utilization report for purposes of monitoring and audit, and ensure that CAs must be supported with complete documentation; and
Submit immediately the related SEF DVs for CY 2023.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly comply with the requirements of submission of PO and their supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within five working days upon its issuance pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001, and
Require the concerned officials or employees to provide a written justification as to non- submission of POs and their supporting documents to COA within the prescribed period, for the whole year of 2023.

We recommended that Management:
a.	Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-001 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01;
b.	Require the concerned Officials and employees, particularly the GAD Focal Point person, to diligently observe the timely submission of GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to COA, as well as in other government agencies such as the DILG; and
c.	Require the Municipal Accountant to promptly submit the GAD related DVs to COA for the Audit Team to have an ample time to review the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO, Human Resource Management (HRM) Officer and the Municipal Budget Officer to initiate the creation and filling up of the mandatory positions prescribed under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC 2014-1 to properly institutionalize the organization of the LDRRM Office and to have continuity in service and implementation of its programs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention on the preparation and submission of the required BRS, prepare subsidiary ledgers per bank accounts, review the records relevant to the Cash in Bank account and effect necessary adjustments to correct the amount presented in the financial statements and other documents; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to update religiously the cash books and the data reflected be preferably considered in the preparation of the BRS to ensure that any reconciling items will be accordingly adjusted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Create an Inventory Committee who will conduct a systematic and complete physical count of PPE in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 and ensure timely submission of RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor;
Require the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively, per category on all PPE as recorded in the books of accounts, and continuously coordinate with each other in order to reconcile the discrepancies in the respective PPE accounts; and
Consider the creation of a permanent GSO, who will discharge on a full-time basis the procurement and issuance of supplies and property, maintain updated Property Cards per category on all Agency’s assets as recorded in the list of PPEs of accounting records, and facilitate the proper conduct of the physical count and preparation of the RPCPPE.
For missing PPE items which can no longer be found/located despite great effort, we recommended that Management strictly follow the guidelines of Section 7.0 of COA Circular 2020-006 for the disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items of government agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Reclassify the amounts recorded under Subsidy to Other LGUs account to Due from LGUs, and demand liquidation reports from the recipient barangays to ascertain if the disbursements were made in accordance with the purpose, and only upon their submission will the Due from LGUs be credited as deemed liquidated;
Submit to the Office of the Auditor the Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement/Project Proposals approved by the LCE and the liquidation reports submitted by the recipient barangays; and
Ensure that future transfers to be granted by the Municipality to the barangays or other LGUs, have the documentary requirements under Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before any funds are released.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after perfection for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review. We also recommended that the BAC revisit the provisions of the IRR of the Procurement Law, specifically in employing different alternative modes of procurement, to ensure its compliance at all times.
We also directed the attention of Management to Section 4.0, the Penalty Clause, of COA Circular No. 2009-001, which states that the inability of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements of the same must be subjected to administrative disciplinary action and the transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents may be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the LDRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱2,633,756.01 to the STF in compliance with COA Circular 2012-002, and do the same at the end of every year.

We recommended that Management, through the LDRRM Office:
Monitor the utilization of LDRRMF by focusing on PPAs requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management;
Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all PPAs identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant the goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved; and
Submit the hard copies of the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to the procedures of the aforementioned Circular, to facilitate verification thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Local Finance Committee to fast-track the approval of the revised/amended Municipal Revenue Code and formulate realistic income projections based on statistics of past years’ experiences and profile of local taxpayers and devise effective plan to avoid, mitigate and control the risks of possible non-collection of projected incomes; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to implement collection strategies to further improve revenue assessment and collection, such as:
Conducting and monitoring periodic collection performance to ensure efficient collection of projected income; and
Examining the books, accounts, and other pertinent records of any person, partnership, corporation, or association subject to local taxes, fees and charges in order to ascertain, assess, and collect the correct amount of the tax, fee, or charge.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MPD Officer and the Municipal Engineer to make representations with the PRDP for the approval of the MOA for the FMR-counterpart, and to immediately implement the Development of Boulevard and Development of Mapitogo projects as embodied in the Municipality’s Investment Plan.
We also recommended that Management judiciously plan the PPAs to be funded out of the 20 per cent DF and ensure its full utilization for the benefit of the constituents and local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020. We also gave emphasis on the provision of Section 3.2.3 of the aforementioned JMC which states that PPAs to be funded out of the 20 per cent DF must be well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to update GAD database to provide accurate information and bases for an effective gender planning, programming and policy formulation; and utilize the GAD Fund only for acceptable PPAs that would address the identified gender issues in the locality based on RA No. 9710 Magna Carta for Women, PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 and other relevant international commitments.
We also recommended that Management consider attribution of a portion or the whole of the Agency’s major programs to the GAD Budget. As provided in PCW Memorandum Circular 2020-05, agencies are advised to use the tool for budget attribution found in Section 1.2.2.2 of the same Circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the concerned personnel, particularly the Supply/Property Officer to prepare and submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor, within 10 days of the following month, the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and of Official Travels in accordance with government rules and regulations; and
Ensure that all travels using government vehicle be controlled through the preparation of the properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets, which must be serially numbered. These accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets must be attached to the disbursement vouchers for the payment of fuel, oil and lubricants. Furthermore, it is then imperative that the Agency must comply with the rules and regulations on fuel consumption and keep its records in detail, as may be necessary, so that correct and accurate information will be provided.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Analyze the affected accounts to determine to which funds the amounts are due and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of each account in their respective fund classification; and
Conduct periodic reconciliation/analysis, preferably on a quarterly basis, of the reciprocal accounts “Due from/Due to Other Funds” to ensure that necessary adjustments in the books of accounts are made on time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Require that an actual inventory-taking of all assets or PPEs of the LGU be conducted each year pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs; that the relevant RPCPPE be submitted not later than January 31 of each year to the Office of the Auditor and that the same be reconciled with the records of the Municipal Accountant;

Require the GSO-designate and the Municipal Accountant-designate to maintain property cards and PPELC, respectively, for all the Agency’s PPEs, to properly monitor their receipts, issuances and balances; and

Require the GSO-designate to maintain ARE for every issuance of equipment to end-users, which will be renewed every three months or every time there is a change in custodianship/user of the party.

Direct officers and employees with cash advances to immediately submit their liquidation papers, otherwise, require the immediate withholding of salary of those officers and employees who were unable to comply with the requirement; 

Require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding cash advances and send written demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; and

Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated cash advances and take legal actions against former employees, who are no longer in the government service, with unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO-designate to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs on the proper recording and reporting of transactions, which requires that all regular purchases of supplies must be booked through the inventory account and the SSMI is needed as a basis for recording the expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts;

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-designate to maintain the SLC and stock cards for each supply and materials, to monitor and control the status of the stocks on hand; and
